title: Analogy Quality — Simple, Relatable, Non-Distracting
description: Analogies must be simple, everyday, and relatable, and must clarify the concept without distracting. No elaborate, niche, or multi-part analogies that need their own explanation.
criterion: 1-2 sentences, everyday domain, maps cleanly to the concept, does not introduce new confusion; no analogy repeated across slides.

rubric.5: Simple, relatable, illuminating, one clean mapping.
rubric.3: Works but slightly stretched or a touch long.
rubric.1: Complex/niche/distracting, or needs its own explanation, or repeated.

input.analogy: Like registered post — you get a delivery receipt for every letter.
Like a Formula-1 team's tyre-degradation model balancing undercut strategy against a virtual safety car window while managing ERS deployment.

cases.rationale: Niche, complex, needs its own explanation — distracting.

input.note: identical postcard analogy already used on an earlier slide for IP

cases.rationale: Repeated analogy across slides — not allowed.
